tweets.full_text:
grok 5 is not just an ai launch

it is a stress test for the whole frontier race

musk is saying 10% chance of agi

but under the hood it is way messier

gigawatt-scale compute
colossus 2
half a million+ gpus
7 models training in parallel
real-time x data
native video
          
huge context
agentic workflows

absolute monster setup

but also

q1 missed
q2 shaky
$1b/month burn
10 of 11 founders gone
active safety scandals
environmental lawsuits
and no public model version clarity

this is the most xai thing possible

insane execution
insane risk
insane narrative

my take:

grok 5 will probably matter

not because it magically becomes agi

but because it may own a very specific lane

real-time intelligence
social signal
fast iteration
cheap api
agentic workflows

claude still feels safer

gpt still owns the ecosystem

gemini has the google distribution cannon

but grok has the chaos engine

worth testing when it drops

not worth betting your whole stack on today

user.name: Mari
user.description: 20. Backend| Tech| AI| contributor @freecodecamp.

tweets.full_text:
AI+ brand is done. 
Only way out.
Rebrand it with a new name and launch after 3 months.bwith new names and proxy companies.
(It has been the modus operandi anyways)

My cloud GPU bill was $1,900/month

One box killed it

Now I have a rack of them

The box is a NVIDIA DGX Spark. $2,999 at launch

128GB unified memory. 1 petaflop of AI compute. Runs 70B models at full precision

A 4090 gives you 24GB VRAM. The moment a model is fatter - CUDA throws an error and you're back to renting

That's the entire reason to own one

Wire two together over the built-in 200Gbps ConnectX-7 link - 256GB combined memory, up to 405B parameters

Four units - announced at GTC 2026 - support up to 700B with near-linear scaling

$18,796 for four nodes. That's the cheapest way to run frontier-class models locally on the planet

At $1,900/month cloud spend it pays itself back in under 2 months

After that - $1,890 a month that used to leave my business just stays

First year: $22,000 redirected back instead of into someone else's data center

Fine-tuning runs that used to cost $400 each - now free

Sensitive client data that can never touch a public model - handled locally

Cloud pricing teaches you to ration AI. Own the hardware and that hesitation disappears

That's where the money was hiding

Bookmark this

tweets.full_text:
Elon Musk: "Orbital Data Centers will change the world"

"In 36 months, probably closer to 30 months, the most economically compelling place to put AI will be space."

Starship is slashing launch costs to around $100 per kg, unlocking orbital data centers as the next frontier for massive AI compute.

Musk explains that in space you get unlimited free solar power with no atmosphere blocking it, plus easy radiative cooling in the vacuum. No more fighting Earth's power grid limits or massive energy bills for training and running AI models.

He doubles down: "You can mark my words" and "Space based AI is obviously the only way to scale."

tweets.full_text:
🧵 Thread 1/15 

NETRA A2000: India’s First Edge AI System-on-Chip (SoC) 🇮🇳

India’s semiconductor ecosystem has achieved a notable milestone with the unveiling of NETRA A2000, an Edge AI System-on-Chip (SoC) designed by Kerala-based semiconductor startup NetraSemi.

Announced by the Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw, it is India’s first Edge AI SoC and a success story of the Government of India’s Design Linked Incentive (DLI) Scheme.

The announcement marks one of the most significant indigenous chip design achievements under India’s semiconductor push to date.

Why does this matter? 👇

@ajaykraina @aravind @Starboy2079

They're builders who understand the fundamentals.

The tools evolve.

tweets.full_text:
AI models are evolving fast.

But the models that actually matter long term are the ones that can reliably execute difficult tasks without constantly breaking midway.

That’s what makes the launch of Claude Opus 4.8 on https://t.co/feB1uBLwsI genuinely interesting.

Instead of chasing hype alone, this update focuses heavily on practical performance improvements developers and power users actually care about:

✓ Stronger reasoning
✓ Better coding capability
✓ ~4× fewer code defect detection failures
✓ More accurate progress reporting
✓ Improved long-duration task execution

And the important part?

All of this arrives while maintaining the same pricing as Opus 4.7.
That balance between capability and efficiency matters a lot as AI workflows become more integrated into real production environments.

Because modern AI usage is shifting beyond: “ask a question → get an answer”
and toward:
• autonomous execution
• multi-step workflows
• persistent task handling
• production-grade development assistance

The more capable models become at maintaining context, tracking progress objectively, and handling longer independent operations, the closer AI moves toward functioning as a true operational layer instead of a simple assistant.

And now users can access Opus 4.8 directly through both:
 API integrations
 Web Chat on https://t.co/feB1uBLwsI

The competition between frontier AI models is no longer just about benchmark scores.

It’s becoming about reliability under real workloads.
And upgrades like this push that transition even further.

#BAI #Web3 #TRONEcoStar 
@BAI_AGI @justinsuntron

かねてより噂のあった初のNVIDIA製『Windows on Arm』チップをMediatekと共同開発し2026/6/1(月)の台北での講演で発表濃厚な模様で、デスクトップ並みの本格AIパソコン仕様のようである。

Microsoft視点では、AppleがArmに全振りしたことが功を奏していることへの対抗だと思料。

MicrosoftやNVIDIA等世界の時価総額トップ企業でさえArmに寄せないと5年後には死活問題になる可能性が充分あると経営陣が判断しての布石と見る。

quoted_status.full_text:
ARMがAI革命の中核になる確度が非常に高いと思う解説①

CPU命令セットの互換性の観点からもAppleが全ての端末（エッジAI）を2020年にARMアーキテクチャーに変更。

これによりAppleは独自のエコシステムと顧客満足度を獲た。

エッジAIでARMの占有率が高い。
これは世の中の縮図と捉えることができる https://t.co/dDPoaHIM9y
